text.title: Agency-to-Agency Referral Program Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Agency-to-Agency Referral Program application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Send ≥3 qualified referrals to a peer BEFORE asking for any. Track in shared sheet so asymmetry is visible.
rule.rationale: Cialdini, Influence ch.2 (reciprocity): unilateral giving builds debt; asking before giving signals neediness.

rule.statement: Choose ONE economics model per peer: flat 10-20% finder's fee, reciprocity-only, OR co-delivery with shared margin. Mixing models breaks trust.
rule.rationale: Mixed models lead to opportunistic re-pricing per deal; explicit policy creates predictability.

rule.statement: Every referred engagement includes anti-poach: receiving agency will not solicit referrer's clients; referrer will not solicit receiving agency's team; ≥18 months.
rule.rationale: Standard agency MSA practice; absence guarantees one bad incident kills the relationship permanently.

rule.statement: Touch the roster ≥4×/year: one founder call (15-30 min), one shared-insight email, one shared content cross-post, one in-person event/dinner where feasible.
rule.rationale: Referrals decay without contact; quarterly cadence is the floor for top-of-mind without spam.

rule.statement: Once/year, drop peers who sent zero AND received zero (after ≥3 referrals sent). Replace with new candidates.
rule.rationale: Time on roster is scarce; dead relationships compound and crowd out warm ones.
